title: Two branches in a conditional structure should avoid having exactly the same implementation
description: Two branches in a conditional structure should not have exactly the same implementationAnalyze your codeadaptability-distinctmaintainabilityCode SmelldesignsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?More InfoWhen the same code is duplicated in two or more separate branches of a conditional, it can make the code harder to understand, maintain, and can potentially introduce bugs if one instance of the code is changed but others are not.Having twocasesin aswitchstatement or two branches in anifchain with the same implementation is at best duplicate code, and at worst a coding error.if (a >= 0 && a < 10) { DoFirst(); DoTheThing(); } else if (a >= 10 && a < 20) { DoTheOtherThing(); } else if (a >= 20 && a < 50) // Noncompliant; duplicates first condition { DoFirst(); DoTheThing(); }switch (i) { case 1: DoF
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Custom function analyzes if/else and switch branches for identical implementations

conditions: Two or more branches have identical code blocks
Not excluded by comments or test contexts

logic.reasoning: Duplicate branches indicate copy-paste errors or missed refactoring opportunities. When two branches have identical implementations, they should be merged into a single branch with combined conditions to improve maintainability.

should_trigger: switch (x) { case 1: Process(); break; case 3: Process(); break; }
should_not_trigger: switch (x) { case 1: Init(); break; case 2: Process(); break; }
